methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 2 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Astro Mechanica is a Series A-stage supersonic and hypersonic company founded by Ian Brooke that has raised $27M+ to date. The relevant comp universe is narrow: two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and two private peers (Boom Supersonic, Hermeus) operate in the same sector, though direct stage and valuation comparables are scarce.

A stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, given the absence of sufficient narrow-set peer data. Using total capital raised ($27M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a standard 2.0x multiplier for Series A to next-stage progression yields $54M. A sensitivity band around this central estimate—accounting for uncertainty in the baseline and step-up assumptions—supports a range of $46M (low) to $62M (high).

The Peer-Median method is not applicable here; while two same-sector peers exist, neither filters into same-stage or adjacent-stage cohorts with sufficient datapoints (minimum three required). The stage-progression result therefore stands as the primary quantitative anchor. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for this particular sub-sector, the precision of the $27M total-raised proxy as a valuation input, and the absence of detailed comparable transaction multiples. A post-money valuation in the $46M–$62M range, centered on $54M, appears supportable given current data constraints.
